transcript.text: On 275 and 285. Also in stereo on VHF. This is National Radio 1.
Well, hello there. TV on the radio here. Thomas the Vance. And welcome once again to the Friday Rock Show.
And this evening we broadcast two of the great groups who helped to make Reading a massive success this year.
Samson from London and those Wolverhampton wild men Slade who re-emerged from the smaller clubs onto the big stage and knocked everybody out.
And we've got classic cuts from Jethro Tull, Fleetwood Mac, Whitesnake, The Pink Floyd, among others, and new stuff by Vardis and by Moore and by Taurus.
Those two latter tracks coming from Metal Inc. Explosion, an album that's coming out next week, which features sessions from the Friday Rock Show.
We've also got the Friday Night Connection, but this evening we start with this.
Looking forward to seeing that band on Monday night when they perform in London.
[Music]
Generally music and love, I suggest.
And before that, you heard something from Highway to Hell by ACDC.
The track was called Walk All Over You.
And that was for Jane and for Chris and for Matt and for Mark and for Tim and for Nicky
and also for Jane and for Chris.
I hope you've got good results in your O-Levels and A-Levels
with lots of love from Steph, who lives in Chilham in Kent.
And also for everybody who attends, I think it's the Malham Public School,
particularly James and Andrew and Warren.
And for Gary and Nick and Rick and Steve and Paul C
and everybody from 4E and 4C of Ellis Gulls Ford School.
i think it is that's a comprehensive school with love from julie the head bangerette mason
and also for all the good friends of adam kinnisley from cheshire and for somebody i
spoke to on the phone today martin hyder and brother chris and all his mates and now here's
something quite old a couple of classics there that of course is jethro tull from the best of
[Music]
jethro tull and living in the past 20 minutes after 10 on the friday rod show that was specially
for Mike Burns, who lives in Dalmalley
up on the west coast of Scotland.
Happy birthday
from Joy. Also, a nice
little letter from Pete Shaw here, who lives in
Brentford and Middlesex, who says, Dear Tom, could you please
give a mention to the Genesis Picnic?
That is happening, it seems, tomorrow,
the 6th of September, at 2pm
at the Albert Memorial in Hyde Park.
Could you also send greetings to all
Genesis loonies who camped out all over
the country? Hope to see you on the 6th.
Can't get there because, of course, I'm going to be working
on Rock on Saturday, but I hope the weather's good
for all of you who congregate around the Albert Memorial in Hyde Park tomorrow for the Genesis
picnic. And living in the past was also for Gillian Thomas. Jill Thomas, let's not be
formal. She lives in Devizes in Wilshire. And prior to that, you heard something, of
course, by Fleetwood Mac from that monster-selling album, Rumours. The track was called Dreams.
And that was for the sister, whose name I don't have, of a gentleman by the name of
Phil. Sister of Phil, you live in Barnsley, and a happy birthday for, what, two days
ago white snake everybody who likes the pink floyd in our country that of course is from the
wall you heard a bit of empty spaces and then the whole of young lust and before that a classic by
white snake ain't no love in the heart of the city and that was for dob who also is known as
robin russell who lives in shore lane in sheffield with all his mates including mosey and phil and
gordon and big campbell and it was for everybody who was at reading particularly i think it's
Carol and Sue from Chelmsford in Essex.
With best wishes from a gentleman called Ollie Barks,
who lives in Morton-in-the-Marsh in Gloucester.
That's a beautiful town, that place.
And also I played it for Chucks, also known as Ian,
and Brian and Jeff and Denise from Nottingham.
And they received good wishes from Pauline and Miriam,
alias The Principal, and Yeti from South Shields.
And I got a nice letter here from Mrs. Anne Yeoman
of 50 Privet House, Cumberland Street, Portsmouth, in Hance,
who wanted me to play a record by Whitesnake
which happened to be Ain't No Love in the Heart of the City
for her daughter Tracy
who lives, I think it is, in Gatley near Manchester
and also for husband Roger's Roger
it's the anniversary of your marriage on September 23rd
and not forgetting Mark
who lives down there in Pompey.
You're listening to the Friday Rock Show from Radio 1
it's twenty-eight minutes before eleven
and now we're going to broadcast
a little storming scent here
that was recorded at Reading by a band called Samson.
I love a Samson, recorded up there at the Reading Festival
a couple of weeks ago, and the tracks you heard were
Hammerhead, Walking Out On You, Vice Versa,
Hard Times, and Take It Like A Man.
Four-piece band, as if you didn't know,
Paul Samson, Bruce Bruce, Chris Aylmer, and Thunderstick,
the masked man behind the drums.
And incidentally, they're going to be at least two of them are,
that's Thunderstick and Bruce Bruce,
on Rock On Saturday tomorrow afternoon.
The mix was by Robbo, the Maida Vale Madman
and production was by Tony Wilson
It's three minutes now to eleven
On the Friday Rock Show from BBC Radio 1
from the BBC with the matrix number REH397
on BBC Records
coming out next week is going to be an album called Metal Explosion
featuring Samson, Praying Mantis, Trespass, Taurus, Angel Witch, Gillen, Money and more
and all the tracks on the album have been broadcast on this programme
and were recorded specially for the programme.
In other words, they were sessions.
And I'm going to play you a couple of cuts now.
The first one's by Moore, and the second one is by Taurus.
This one's called Soldier.
A couple of tracks that are going to be released on an album,
which comes out next week.
You heard there Taurus and Paper Chaser.
Before that, it was Moore and Soldier.
And the album comes out next week on BBC Records and Tapes.
And it's called Metal Explosion.
Nine minutes after 11, and it's time for the Friday Night Connection.
and also a long-winded explanation which I'll try to praise and keep relatively short
because we like to play a lot of music on this program, right?
The tracks we played you last week were Gypsy Eyes by Jimi Hendrix
from the LP The Essential Jimi Hendrix.
That's the LP we played it all.
Souped Up 4 by Rory Gallagher from the LP Against the Grain
and Planet Caliban by Black Sabbath from Paranoid.
Now, the answer as set was that all the guitarists were left-handed.
Now, we actually believe that to be incorrect.
So what we've done, in our infinite wisdom, fools that we are,
we've scrapped the whole of that connection, and we didn't get too many entries this week, obviously.
So not too many people have lost 12p stamps, and we're sorry.
Basically, that's what I'm trying to say.
In other words, will you accept, please, that last week's connection was null and void,
because we don't believe that the answer was absolutely correct.
We could, really, to be honest, have codded it up and given you a load of different explanations
and found another connection, but we thought rather than do that, we'd tell you the truth.
Because that really is what we're all about.
So what I've done this week, if you'll excuse me, is to put together a Friday night connection myself.
Do I get the £10 record token? Of course I don't.
For making the mistake, I've got to give them £10.
Wilson is standing over me now, about to collect it.
This connection was put together by Tommy Vance.
It's not that difficult, but you'll have to think about it.
It's in there, somewhere.
That's my Friday night connection, set specially for you.
[Music]
And really, it was an excuse just to play three of the greatest tracks that I know.
Three tracks that I just keep on playing at home again and again and again.
But there is a definite connection in it.
And if you think you know what it is, jot it down on a postcard and send it to the Friday Night Connection,
the Friday Rock Show, Radio 1, London, W1A4WW.
And if you get it all right, and we'll send you a £10 record token,
don't forget to put together your own Friday Night Connection as well.
Sorry about the muck-up last week, but better to tell you the truth, who thinks?
okay now let's return to our recorded live from redding set and i think one of the biggest
surprises was slayed when they hit the stage now they're a band who've been on the small circuit
for a long time haven't been on a big stage for some considerable years really when they went on
the stage they were received warmly as you will hear right here and that for me was one of the
[Music]
magic moments in rock and roll that i will never ever forget and i've been in rock and roll on and
for quite a number of years and that was just a magic moment the re-emergence of slade to a big
big stage and the tracks that you heard were come on feel the noise and also merry christmas but
then the crowd sang that get down and get with it mama we're all crazy now walk alone sung again by
the reading crowd something else else medley with all various bits and pieces that they put in there
the wheels ain't coming down that's a new song of theirs when i'm dancing which is also a new song
that I'd not heard before, and then take me back home.
And as I say, it was just a magic moment.
The right band on stage at the right time,
and an audience that were so receptive.
I think the lads are on the way to Belgium at the moment.
Slade, thank you very much for that set.
I think you're down in Dover at the moment. Have a good time.
It was magic.
Next week, in our Live from Reading series,
Def Leppard and Ian Gillan.
And on Rock on Saturday, we'll have Kiss.
This, of course, is tomorrow.
Samson
The Teardrop Explodes
tracks from their LP
and Lemmy from Motory
programs produced by
Tony Wilson
Wilson speaks
safe safe safe
I'm not here
I'm on holiday tonight
oh alright go away
following the pips
the Radio 1 transmitters
that's 275285
we're going to close down
but they'll be back with you
on the air tomorrow morning
at 5 o'clock
and this is Tommy Vance
on behalf of all of us
on Radio 1
wishing you well
God bless and good night
